original: NASA successfully flies its drone helicopter Ingenuity on Mars, first powered aircraft to fly on another world
Larry Walters, American "lawn chair" pilot, born in Los Angeles, California (d. 1993)
American Revolution begins in Lexington, Massachusetts, with the "Shot Heard Round the World" fired later that day in Concord
Sally Rideis named the 1st American woman astronaut
Guinon Bluford announced as 1st African American NASA astronaut
Simu Liu, Canadian actor (Kim's Convenience, Shang-Chi And The Legend Of The Ten Rings), born in Harbin, China
Nemesis, death star of dinosaurs 1st appears in print (Nature magazine)
Last wild condor captured on California wildlife reserve

British explorer CaptainJames Cookfirst sights Australia. Writes in his log book that “what we have as yet seen of this land appears rather low, and not very hilly, the face of the Country green and Woody, but the Sea shore is all a white Sand.”
Paul Revere, William Dawes and Samuel Prescott are captured by British troops riding from Lexington to Concord, Prescott escapes to warn Concord as does Dawes who acts as a decoy
USSR Salyut 1 launched; 1st manned lab in orbit
NASA civilian pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 to 46,900m
India launches 1st satellite with help of USSR
Shirley Templeappears in her 1st feature length film, "Stand Up & Cheer"
The Beatles sign a contract agreeing to stay together for 10 years (they don't)
Johnny Cashreleases single "Ring of Fire" written by his future wifeJune Carterand Merle Kilgore
US SenatorTammy Duckworth(Illinois-D) is the first parent to bring a baby into the Senate chambers, a day after the Senate votes to allow babies on the chamber's floor
Candace Parker, American basketball player (Los Angeles Sparks), born in St. Louis, Missouri
Evander Holyfieldbeats George Foreman in 12 for heavyweight boxing title
Eliot Ness, US Federal agent "The Untouchables" (put away Al Capone), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1957)
Tim Curry, English actor (Rocky Horror Picture Show, Star Wars: The Clone Wars), born in Grappenhall, Cheshire, England
AAU record for a 25-foot rope climb is set in 4.7 seconds
Jacqueline Blanc, sets women's downhill ski speed rec (124.902 mph)
1st Boston Marathon (B.A.A. Road Race), won by John J. McDermott in 2:55:10; the world's oldest annual marathon inspired by success of the first marathon at the 1896 Summer Olympics
SF Earthquake ends killing 452
Graeme Obree bicycles world record 10km (11:25.88)
Glenn T. Seaborg, American chemist (AEC, Plutonium, Nobel 1951), born in Ishpeming, Michigan (d. 1999)
Loren Gray, American singer and TikTok star, born in Pottstown, Pennsylvania
Charles Duryea takes the 1st American-made automotive for a test drive
Baseball uniforms begin displaying players' names on their backs
Troy Polamalu, American football player (Pittsburgh Steelers), born in Garden Grove, California
Joe Mauer, American baseball player (Minnesota Twins), born in St. Paul, Minnesota
16th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (51-D): Discovery 4 returns to Earth
USSR Salyut 7 space station put into orbit
Comet 1P/607 H1 (Halley) approaches within 0.0898 AUs of Earth
John Adamssecures Dutch Republic's recognition of the United States as an independent government, a house he purchased in The Hague becomes America's first embassy
Ole Evinrude, Norwegian-American industrialist and inventor (outboard marine engine), born in Gjøvik, Norway (d. 1934)

British banker Thomas Agnew and others found the Liverpool Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children (LSPCC), based on Agnew's observation of a similar group in NYC; its success leads to a national organization
Sir Alexander Ogston, British surgeon (discovered Staphylococcus), educator, and military doctor, born in Aberdeen, Scotland (d. 1929)

Jason Gillespie, Australian cricket fast bowler (first Aboriginal male to play Test cricket for Australia; 71 Tests, 259 wickets, BB 7/39, HS 201no; South Australia CA, Yorkshire CCC, Sussex CCC), born in Sydney, Australia
Joe Hart, English soccer goalkeeper (75 caps; Manchester City 266 games), born in Shrewsbury, England
Rivaldo, Brazilian footballer (Brazil national team 1993-2003), born in Recife, Brazil
Temoc Suarez, American soccer forward (Olympic gold 1996), born in Greenwood, South Carolina
Brendon Kuruppu scores 201* on Test Cricket debut (Sri Lanka v NZ)
Suh Yun Buck wins world record marathon (2:25:39)
In Las Vegas, Nevada: San Jose Sharks forward Patrick Marleau breaks Gordie Howe's NHL record for most games played, now at 1,768 and counting
Michael Spinks beatsLarry Holmesin 15 for heavyweight boxing title
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, Ontario: Toronto Maple Leafs edge Montreal Canadiens, 2-1 for a 4-2 series win; first all-Canadian final since 1935
NFL Draft: Ohio State offensive tackle Orlando Pace first pick by St. Louis Rams
1st MLB baseball game in New Jersey - Brooklyn Dodgers beat Philadelphia Phillies, 5-4, at Roosevelt Stadium in Jersey City
Yankees dedicate a plaque forBabe Ruth
British rock band Queen begins concert tour of Japan
Bernie Worrell, American R&B and funk keyboardist (Funkadelic - "Cosmic Slop"), born in Long Branch, New Jersey (d. 2016)
Earl Gardner, American jazz trumpeter (Thad Jones/Mel Lewis Orchestra; Saturday Night Live, 1985-2022; Carla Bley), born in New York City
Belinda Lipscomb, American vocalist (Midnight Star - "No Parking On The Dance Floor"; "Operator"), born in Louisville, Kentucky
Ge You, Chinese Actor (best actor Cannes 1994 "To Live"), born in Beijing, China
Ruud Jolie, Dutch symphonic metal guitarist (Within Temptation), born in Tilburg, Netherlands
Dar Williams, American singer-songwriter ("You're Aging Well"; "When I Was A Boy"; The Christians and The Pagans"), born in Mount Kisco, New York
Arshad Warsi, Indian film actor (Lage Raho Munna Bhai), born in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India
Amanda Sage, American visionary artist, born in Denver, Colorado
